summary: U.S. equities extended their rally into record territory Friday as the S&P 500 and Nasdaq posted their sixth consecutive weekly gain, propelled by better-than-expected April jobs data and resilient earnings despite persistent geopolitical tensions. The economy added 115,000 jobs in April, nearly doubling the 65,000 consensus and unemployment held steady at 4.3%, signaling economic resilience even as energy prices remain elevated due to the Iran conflict. AMD led the earnings charge with a massive revenue beat that sent shares up 20% for the week, crossing the $700 billion market cap threshold as AI infrastructure demand accelerates. Meanwhile, crypto markets held steady above key support levels with Bitcoin trading around $80,000 and Ethereum near $2,300, though a brief AWS outage disrupted Coinbase and FanDuel trading Thursday night. The Fed's late-April decision to hold rates at 3.5-3.75% continues to frame market expectations, with Chair Powell planning to remain as a Fed governor even after Kevin Warsh takes over the chairmanship on May 15. Oil volatility persists with WTI near $95 and Brent above $100 as strikes between the U.S. and Iran undermined ceasefire hopes, keeping inflation concerns front and center.

signals.title: April jobs report doubles expectations at 115K additions
signals.desc: U.S. payrolls beat consensus by nearly 2x with unemployment steady at 4.3%, easing recession fears and supporting equity rally to records.

signals.title: AMD crushes Q1 with $10.25B revenue, shares surge 20% weekly
signals.desc: Chipmaker's AI-driven data center revenue up 57% YoY, market cap crosses $700B as demand for accelerators and CPUs accelerates.

signals.title: Oil spikes above $100 as U.S.-Iran strikes violate fragile ceasefire
signals.desc: Brent crude hit $119 intraday Thursday before settling near $101, reigniting stagflation concerns and weighing on consumer sentiment.

signals.title: Fed holds rates at 3.5-3.75% amid 8-4 split vote and leadership transition
signals.desc: Powell stays as governor post-chairmanship; most dissents since 1992 signal deep uncertainty over inflation vs. growth trade-off.

signals.title: Bitcoin holds $80K as AWS outage disrupts Coinbase trading overnight
signals.desc: Thermal issue at Northern Virginia data center halted crypto exchange services for hours; BTC and ETH steady despite infrastructure hiccup.

verdict: Risk-on momentum persists on jobs strength and AI earnings, but oil above $100 and Fed uncertainty cloud the path forward.
